description: §35 — a nested `let` declaration that shadows an in-scope NON-`lin` variable of the same name does NOT fire E-LIN-005. Structurally identical to lin-005-shadow-lin-pos (an outer `token` binding consumed once, then a nested `if` body redeclaring `let token = 5`), but the outer `token` is a plain `let`, not `lin`. E-LIN-005 is conditioned on the shadowed binding being LINEAR; shadowing an ordinary binding is legal lexical scoping and SHALL NOT be rejected. Isolates the trigger to lin-ness. W-PROGRAM-001 co-fires incidentally (no `<program>` shell); not asserted.
rationale: §35 registry (E-LIN-005) is the lin-shadowing rule: the reject applies specifically to shadowing an in-scope `lin` variable. type-system.ts checkLinShadowing (:7737) returns early unless `scopeChain.lookup(name).isLin` is true — a non-`lin` outer binding never matches. Ordinary lexical shadowing (`let` over `let`) is permitted; only the linear-binding shadow is unsound (it defeats exactly-once consumption attribution). Here the outer `token` is a plain `let`, so E-LIN-005 SHALL stay silent.
